---
title: "Let me sleep!"
author: "Hanlin Su"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
```

```{css}

```

## Project requirements
![A photo of current dir](p1image.PNG)
![A link to a github](https://github.com/gooosey/stats220)

## Inspo meme
I choose this meme because I relate to this, sleeping late and texting friends. # give me some more details
![A gif of a duck going back to bed and a text at the bottom of the gif saying "I sleep now, you sleep soon"](inspo_meme.gif)

## My meme
The inspo meme I choose was a meme that I can relate to. I have changed the size of the meme because the inspo meme was too big and I didn't like how it looked when adding my text "Good night chat". I placed this text at the top because any other places it wouldn't look right.
!["A png of a duck going back to bed, at the top of the image we have a text saying "Good night chat" and a text at the bottom of the gif saying "I sleep now, you sleep soon"](my_meme.png)
## My animated meme 
!["A animated png of a duck with text top saying "Good night chat..." and a text at the bottom saying "I sleep now, you sleep soon" and meme slowly going dark](animated_meme.gif)

## Creativity
  

## Learning reflection


## Appendix

<mark>Do not change, edit, or remove the `R` chunk included below.</mark> 

If you are working within RStudio and within your Project1 RStudio project (check the top right-hand corner says "Project1"), then the code from the `meme.R` script will be displayed below.

This code needs to be visible for your project to be marked appropriately, as some of the criteria are based on this code being submitted.


```{r file='meme.R', eval=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

```
